chunks.page_summary: This document provides step-by-step instructions on how to configure the Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) software to integrate and utilize an external pressure sensor for measurement and data logging.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
External Pressure Sensor-How does it work with the RCP software?
Modified on: Thu, 25 Aug, 2022 at 4:44 AM

The external pressure library works to retrieve the pressure measurements from an external pressure sensor, and write the pressure value back to the SME.

The followings steps describe the guidelines to activate the External Pressure sensor readings:

1. In the measurements tab, in status, the manual entry must be disabled

Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.2
Measurement | Events | Settings | Service | Communication | Logger | Help

Measured Values
Calc. Visc: not connected
Density: not connected
Temperature: not connected
Pressure: not connected

Status
Error: not connected E99
Logging: Stopped
Pressure: Select Channel
Manual entry: [checkbox] (87 psi)

Log Measurements
Sample Name:
Start Logging | Add Event

Sensor Information
Serial Number: - no sn -
Fres: - df -

Graph 1 (left axis: Calc. Visc [mPa.s], right axis: Density [kg/m3])
  Scale: -1.0000 to 1.0000
  Time: 00:21:30 to 00:23:15
Graph 2 (left axis: Temperature [°C], right axis: Pressure [psi])
  Scale: -1.0000 to 1.0000 (Temp), -15 to 15 (Pressure)
  Time: 00:21:30 to 00:23:15

Figure 1. Disabling Manual Entry for Pressure.

2. Then in settings tab, in Pressure Gauge communication, select External Pressure.

Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.2
Measurement | Events | Settings | Service | Communication | Logger | Help

Operator
Name / ID: Rheonics
Client:

Sensor Configuration
Sensor Type: - no sensor type -
Import | Export | Expert Mode

Communication
USB | Port: COM8 | Autodetect | Apply | Device Manager | Status (red dot)
Ethernet | IP: 10.100.0.18 | Port: 10001
Bluetooth

Units
Viscosity: mPa.s | cP | cSt | Resolution: 0.0000
Density: g/cc | kg/m3 | lb/gal | lb/ft3 | Resolution: 0.0000
Temperature: °C | °K | °F | Resolution: 0.0000
Pressure: bar | kPa | MPa | psi | torr | atm | Resolution: 0

Pressure Gauge
Communication: External Sensor (selected)
Device ID: Calc. Visc

Graph Settings
Calc. Visc: auto / fix | Min: 1 mPa.s | Max: 40 mPa.s
Calc. Density: auto / fix | Min: 0 kg/m3 | Max: 1.5 kg/m3
Temperature Scale: auto / fix | Min: 10 °C | Max: 60 °C
Pressure Scale: auto / fix | Min: 14.504 psi | Max: 145.04 psi
Time Scale: full / fix | Range: 5 min
Graph Interval: realtime / same as logging
[Checkbox] Plot df (damping) and Fres (resonant frequency) instead of Viscosity & Density

Log Settings
Logging Interval: 10 sec
Measurement Frequency (Every): 5 sec
[Checkbox] Log and display data with error code
[Checkbox] Log debug information: 1 min

Figure 2. Selecting Communication with External Sensor

3. When selected. The software will prompt for the path of the external pressure Library. By default it will look for it in the C:\ProgramData\RheonicsCont
key_facts: The external pressure library is designed to retrieve pressure measurements from an external sensor and transmit them to the SME.
To activate external pressure sensor readings, the manual entry option for pressure in the 'Measurement' tab must be disabled.
The 'External Sensor' option must be selected under 'Pressure Gauge' communication in the 'Settings' tab.
Upon selecting 'External Sensor' communication, the software will prompt the user to specify the path for the external pressure library.
By default, the software expects to find the external pressure library in the 'C:\ProgramData\RheonicsCont' directory.
diagram_labels: Figure 1. Disabling Manual Entry for Pressure.
Plot df (damping) and Fres (resonant frequency) instead of Viscosity & Density
Log and display data with error code
Figure 2. Selecting Communication with External Sensor

chunks.page_summary: This page details the configuration process for an external pressure sensor using the Rheonics Control Panel software, covering communication settings, device identification, and the display and logging of real-time measurements.
chunks.extracted_text:

Figure 4. When selecting a correct .llb file the Configuration appears.

5. Once the external .llb file of the library is selected. The library will execute a first VI that will prompt for the communication settings. In the case of Addi

When the sensor is correctly set the device ID is then displayed on the settings tab:

Pressure Gauge
Communication: External Sensor [dropdown]
Device ID: Additel 681

Figure 5. Pressure Gauge with Custom Library

And the pressure gauge will start taking measurements, which will be displayed in the measurements tab. These will also be stored in the logfiles.

Measurement Tab (Figure 6)
Measured Values
  Calc. Visc: not connected
  Density: not connected
  Temperature: not connected
  Pressure: NaN psi

Status
  Status: [red indicator] Error: not connected E99
  Logging: Stopped
  Pressure: External Sensor [dropdown]
  [Checkbox] Manual entry: 87 psi

Log Measurements
  Sample Name: [empty text box]
  [Button] Start Logging
  [Button] Add Event

Sensor Information
  Serial Number: - no sn -
  Fres: -
  df: -

Graphs Displayed:
  Calc. Visc [mPa.s] vs hh:mm:ss
  Density [kg/m3] vs hh:mm:ss
  Temperature [°C] vs hh:mm:ss
  Pressure [psi] vs hh:mm:ss

Figure 6. Measurement tab with external pressure sensor active.
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.2 software is used to configure and monitor sensors.
Selecting an external .llb library file activates a configuration interface for the pressure sensor.
The software prompts for communication settings via a VI upon selecting the library file.
The device ID of the configured sensor (e.g., Additel 681) is displayed in the settings tab.
Once configured, the pressure gauge begins taking measurements.
Measurements for viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure are displayed in the 'Measurement' tab.
All measurements are automatically stored in logfiles.
The system provides status indicators, including 'Error: not connected E99' when sensors are inactive or disconnected.
Users can adjust logging intervals, measurement frequency, and graph display scales.
The software can plot damping (df) and resonant frequency (Fres) instead of viscosity and density.
diagram_labels: Plot df (damping) and Fres (resonant frequency) instead of Viscosity & Density
Log and display data with error code
warnings_notes: Status indicator shows red dot and 'Error: not connected E99' when sensors are not active or connected, indicating a potential issue.
